description:
You are an observability engineer who has been asked to migrate the historical «metrics» table from the legacy SQLite database to a new database file and then validate that the migration was loss-less.

PRE-EXISTING MATERIAL
1. Legacy DB: /home/user/observability.db  
   • Contains exactly one table named metrics (schema: id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, value REAL).  
   • The table currently holds exactly three rows.

GOAL
1. Create a brand-new SQLite database file called /home/user/observability_v2.db.  
2. Copy (migrate) the entire metrics table—including its schema and the three existing rows—from the legacy DB into this new DB.  
3. Verify that the row-count of metrics in the new DB matches the row-count in the legacy DB.  
4. Produce a human-readable validation log file at /home/user/migration_validation.log whose exact contents are:

SOURCE_DB=/home/user/observability.db  
DEST_DB=/home/user/observability_v2.db  
SOURCE_ROWS=3  
DEST_ROWS=3  
MIGRATION_STATUS=SUCCESS

Output-format rules for /home/user/migration_validation.log:  
• Five lines, no extra whitespace, exactly as shown above.  
• Each key and value is joined by a single «=».  
• A trailing newline is allowed but not required.

CONSTRAINTS
• Complete the entire task using at most 2–3 shell commands (compound commands separated by «&&» count towards this limit).  
• Do not modify the original /home/user/observability.db.  
• All work must be done from /home/user.  

The automated tests will check:
1. /home/user/observability_v2.db exists and is a valid SQLite database.  
2. The metrics table exists in the new database with the same schema as in the legacy DB.  
3. There are exactly three rows in both the legacy and the new metrics tables.  
4. /home/user/migration_validation.log exists and its contents exactly match the specification above.
truth:
# Pre-created artefacts before the agent starts
1. /home/user/observability.db  
   • SQLite file containing:
     CREATE TABLE metrics (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, value REAL);
     INSERT INTO metrics VALUES
       (1,'cpu_usage',0.75),
       (2,'mem_usage',0.60),
       (3,'disk_io',123.45);

# Expected artefacts after successful completion
1. /home/user/observability_v2.db  
   • Must contain an identical metrics table with the same three rows and no additional tables.
2. /home/user/migration_validation.log  
   Expected EXACT byte-for-byte content (UTF-8):

SOURCE_DB=/home/user/observability.db
DEST_DB=/home/user/observability_v2.db
SOURCE_ROWS=3
DEST_ROWS=3
MIGRATION_STATUS=SUCCESS

(with a single LF after each line; an optional LF after the last line is acceptable.)
